availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

part.delta: I'll bootstrap three peer sessions (planner, builder, reviewer) and let them coordinate through the board
. I'll send identical choreography instructions plus a role hint to each.

params.prompt:
You are a peer participant in a Firegrid choreography experiment. Your role hint: PLANNER (suggest the small improvement and frame it for peers). There is no central manager. Coordinate ONLY through the shared board channels — do not reply to whoever spawned you.

Run id: 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z
Scenario id: solo-baseline
Arm: choreography

Board channels (use Firegrid send/wait_for/wait_for_any):
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Every send to a coordination.* channel must have payload as a JSON OBJECT (not a JSON string).
- Before anyone publishes coordination.final, the board must contain at least one coordination.claims row AND at least one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row. If a final already exists, still publish your claim+finding/review — the runner measures board behavior.
- Do NOT use shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repository-inspection tools. Use ONLY Firegrid session and channel tools. The task packet below is the only material.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars. Make the send call before writing prose. After send returns success, stop.

Suggested choreography:
1. Publish a coordination.claims row claiming the planner slice (kind:"claim", title, workId:"plan", claimantSessionId:"planner").
2. Publish a coordination.findings row with your proposed small doc/workflow improvement (kind:"finding", workId:"plan", body has: evidence inspected, the recommendation, open questions).
3. Watch coordination.findings and coordination.reviews from peers (builder, reviewer) via wait_for_any with a bounded timeout (e.g. 60000 ms).
4. If no coordination.final exists after peers have weighed in (or after timeout), publish coordination.final yourself with the converged recommendation. Otherwise still publish your own finding row.

Task (self-contained — do not inspect repo/shell/fs):
Experiment goal: Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- Driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants launched via public Firegrid client/session surface.
- Shared board exposed as the six coordination.* channels above.
- Driver injects inbound events via Firegrid.channels.send; waits for final via Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- Task packets demanding repo/shell access burn time on unavailable tools.
- Optional-prose final artifacts make scoring unreliable across arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not hidden driver state.

Task: Identify ONE small documentation or workflow improvement using only the provided materials. Return the exact change you would make and why it is enough.

Output contract for coordination.final (only if you are the one to publish it):
- kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", workId:"plan" (or similar)
- body under 1,200 chars
- state evidence inspected, the recommendation, open questions
- do not claim coordination helped unless board artifacts show why

Begin now by publishing your coordination.claims row, then your coordination.findings row, then watch the board.

params.prompt:
You are a peer participant in a Firegrid choreography experiment. Your role hint: BUILDER (concretize the change — exact wording / patch shape — based on planner's finding). There is no central manager. Coordinate ONLY through the shared board channels — do not reply to whoever spawned you.

Run id: 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z
Scenario id: solo-baseline
Arm: choreography

Board channels (use Firegrid send/wait_for/wait_for_any):
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Every send to a coordination.* channel must have payload as a JSON OBJECT (not a JSON string).
- Before anyone publishes coordination.final, the board must contain at least one coordination.claims row AND at least one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row. If a final already exists, still publish your claim+finding/review — the runner measures board behavior.
- Do NOT use shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repository-inspection tools. Use ONLY Firegrid session and channel tools. The task packet below is the only material.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars. Make the send call before writing prose. After send returns success, stop.

Suggested choreography:
1. Wait briefly (wait_for_any on coordination.findings/coordination.claims, timeoutMs ~ 45000) for the planner's finding. If nothing arrives, proceed with your own analysis.
2. Publish a coordination.claims row (kind:"claim", workId:"build", claimantSessionId:"builder").
3. Publish a coordination.findings row that concretizes the change (kind:"finding", workId:"build", body: exact wording / minimal patch shape, why this is sufficient).
4. Watch coordination.reviews; if reviewer approves and no final yet, you may publish coordination.final. Otherwise keep your finding on the board.

Task (self-contained — do not inspect repo/shell/fs):
Experiment goal: Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- Driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants launched via public Firegrid client/session surface.
- Shared board exposed as the six coordination.* channels above.
- Driver injects inbound events via Firegrid.channels.send; waits for final via Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- Task packets demanding repo/shell access burn time on unavailable tools.
- Optional-prose final artifacts make scoring unreliable across arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not hidden driver state.

Task: Identify ONE small documentation or workflow improvement using only the provided materials. Return the exact change you would make and why it is enough.

Output contract for coordination.final (only if you are the one to publish it):
- kind:"final", title, body, status:"complete", workId:"build" (or similar)
- body under 1,200 chars
- state evidence inspected, the recommendation, open questions
- do not claim coordination helped unless board artifacts show why

Begin now by watching the board for planner output, then publishing your claim and finding.

params.prompt:
You are a peer participant in a Firegrid choreography experiment. Your role hint: REVIEWER (validate planner/builder findings against task constraints; publish a coordination.reviews row; optionally publish coordination.final if no peer has and the board has the required preconditions). There is no central manager. Coordinate ONLY through the shared board channels — do not reply to whoever spawned you.

Run id: 2026-05-25T02-28-38-461Z
Scenario id: solo-baseline
Arm: choreography

Board channels (use Firegrid send/wait_for/wait_for_any):
- coordination.work
- coordination.claims
- coordination.findings
- coordination.questions
- coordination.reviews
- coordination.final

Rules:
- Every send to a coordination.* channel must have payload as a JSON OBJECT (not a JSON string).
- Before anyone publishes coordination.final, the board must contain at least one coordination.claims row AND at least one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row. If a final already exists, still publish your claim+review — the runner measures board behavior.
- Do NOT use shell, filesystem, terminal, execute, or repository-inspection tools. Use ONLY Firegrid session and channel tools. The task packet below is the only material.
- Keep coordination.final body under 1,200 chars. Make the send call before writing prose. After send returns success, stop.

Suggested choreography:
1. Watch coordination.findings + coordination.claims via wait_for_any (timeoutMs ~ 75000). Collect at least the planner finding; ideally also the builder finding.
2. Publish a coordination.claims row (kind:"claim", workId:"review", claimantSessionId:"reviewer").
3. Publish a coordination.reviews row (kind:"review", workId:"review", body: which findings you read, whether the proposed change satisfies the output contract under 1,200 chars and addresses one of the named measurement risks, any objections).
4. If no coordination.final exists AND the board now has at least one coordination.claims row plus at least one coordination.findings or coordination.reviews row, you may publish coordination.final summarizing the convergence. Otherwise stop after your review.

part.delta: All three peers spawned. Now I'll observe the board for the final art
ifact (and skim coordination activity along the way) without assigning hidden work.
no coordination.final. Let me check what (if anything) the peers actually put on the board, and observ
e their session output to see if they're stuck.
